title: FWM image sorting CV gate follow-up and sync - 2026-06-04

messages.text: Asked Codex to add the image sorting transcript to the transcripts table, sync all data to S3, and sync the local repository.

Confirmed the FWM repo is using the canonical origin https://github.com/bsinger3/FWM_Repo.git and planned the same delivery pattern used previously: S3 sync for bulk output artifacts, transcript upload through scripts/upload-codex-chat-transcript.mjs, and a scoped Git commit/push for code, docs, small reports, and transcript artifacts.
Previously asked Codex to break down the reasons for rows in the needs-human-review workbooks.

Aggregated 41 needs-human-review workbooks from outputs/02_supabase_needs_human_review_cv_first_pass/partial_170000_rows_cv_gated and found 40,170 review rows: 31,859 BORDERLINE_BODY_COVERAGE, 6,979 BORDERLINE_SUBJECT_SIZE, 669 IMAGE_FETCH_FAILED, and 663 BORDERLINE_COMPOSITION. Verified there were zero CLEAR_PASS approve rows remaining in needs-human-review.
Asked what the difference is between BORDERLINE_BODY_COVERAGE and BORDERLINE_SUBJECT_SIZE.

Explained that BORDERLINE_SUBJECT_SIZE means the person is present but too small or far away in the image, making those rows possible future crop-salvage candidates. BORDERLINE_BODY_COVERAGE means the person is large enough, but useful pose/body visibility is incomplete or uncertain, so cropping is less likely to fix it.

Synced the current CV-gated output package under outputs/02_supabase_needs_human_review_cv_first_pass/partial_170000_rows_cv_gated to S3 at s3://fwm-scraping-data-briannasinger/FWM_Repo/outputs/02_supabase_needs_human_review_cv_first_pass/partial_170000_rows_cv_gated, excluding .DS_Store files.

Uploaded this focused transcript to the Supabase codex_chat_transcripts table using the repo's existing uploader with --skip-openai-summary, then committed and pushed the local repository changes to origin/main.

next_steps: Continue human review from the current approval, disapproval, and needs-human-review workbooks.
Use BORDERLINE_SUBJECT_SIZE rows as the best future candidates for crop-based salvage experiments.
Keep bulky generated workbooks and checkpoints in S3 rather than Git.
